sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 38-year-old female, presents with anxiety regarding recent blood work showing hepatitis C.
She reports being surprised by the diagnosis as she has been feeling fine.
She denies any previous knowledge of having hepatitis C and reports a history of IV drug use in the past, which she has been clean from for over 15 years.
She also reports a history of excessive alcohol use, currently consuming about 1 beer per day and slightly more on weekends.
She admits to smoking, having decreased her intake from 1-2 packs per day to 1-2 cigarettes per day.
She reports being tired at the end of the day, despite getting enough sleep, and attributes this to her work schedule.
She has a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Past medical history: The patient reports a physical examination by her primary care physician (PCP) 6 months ago, which showed no other conditions.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
The heart exam shows a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
The lungs are clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
The abdomen is soft with no hepatosplenomegaly.
The musculoskeletal exam is normal, with no gait disturbance or edema.
Test Results: The patient's HCV antibody test is positive, and her liver panel shows an elevated AST of 39, but normal albumin and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a confirmed diagnosis of hepatitis C, with initial labs consistent with the infection.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; Suspected liver fibrosis.
Plan:
I prescribed a liver ultrasound to determine the severity of the patient's liver disease and an HCV RNA test to confirm the diagnosis.
I also recommended that the patient's family members undergo the same screening as the patient.
The patient will be scheduled for follow-up in 3 weeks.
Follow-up:
The patient will be seen in 3 weeks for follow-up and to discuss the results of the liver ultrasound and HCV RNA test.
